node.writeup: FanDuel. Smart has cleared this line in 3/3 games without Luka Doncic, but with LeBron James and Austin Reaves. I like this spot against the Mavericks who play fast (fourth in pace). And the Dallas defense has cratered without Anthony Davis in the middle, which has lead to more weakside help, and thus, more secondary scoring. Over the last ten games, the Mavs have allowed the seventh most scratch and shoot attempts, and the third most assists.

selection.label: Marcus Smart Over 14.5 Total Points + Assists -108

node.writeup: Playing an Under on Utah generally isn't smart as it is by far the worst defensive team in the league. But the Blazers may have some tired legs in the second of a B2B (tired legs, jumpers are short; this is also at altitude) and are probably without Shaedon Sharpe (21.4 PPG) again. They might sit some other guys in the last game pre-break. Utah will be tanking so hard in this one to avoid a season-high three-game winning streak -- I will touch on this in the newsletter. I am almost positive that Lauri Markkanen sits the second of a B2B and probably several others. This certainly could turn into a defenseless scrimmage with 260 points, but I'll take a half-unit shot.

This is the second game of back-to-back sets for both the Trail Blazers and the Jazz. Scoot Henderson (rest), Shaedon Sharpe (calf) and Robert Williams III (rest) could all sit out for Portland. Vit Krejci has already scored at least 10 points in three of his last four games, despite not playing more than 26 minutes in any of them. The potential for additional minutes and shot attempts against a Jazz team that has the worst defensive rating in the league makes this over appealing. When Krejci faced them as a member of the Hawks earlier in the season, he scored 20 points over 32 minutes.

node.writeup: Caesar’s. This game sets up nicely for Jared McCain, who should be poised for 22-26 minutes off the bench. The Thunder will be without a laundry list of players, and coming off a game last night, this profiles as a spot for the newest Thunder to find his groove. McCain will face a Bucks defense that allows the third most points to spot up shooters.

expertStreaks.label: 93-67 in Last 160 NBA Player Props Picks

node.writeup: Concerning for Milwaukee that OKC was shorthanded (like tonight) when rolling to a 122-102 win at Fiserv Forum on January 21.  More concerning as Giannis was available that night and it didn't prevent OKC from pulling clear.  Giannis, however, has been out the past few weeks, though the Bucks have shown some surprising spunk since last week, winning four of five.  Recent signee, ex-Nets G Cam Thomas, pumped in 34 points in Tuesday's win at Orlando.  Though the Thunder was also minus Jalen Williams and Isaiah Hartenstein for the first meeting, as they will be tonight, they weren't minus SGA, who scored 40 in that easy OKC win, but he's now out for the rematch, so it's a decidedly under-strength Thunder this evening.  Play Bucks

DraftKings. With the Spurs on a back-to-back, and Stephon Castle questionable after leaving the game early last night, I’m once again going to back Dylan Harper on his points plus assists line. He’s cleared this number in five of his last six games. Even if Castle does suit up, there’s enough runway here for the rookie to clear this line against a Warriors defense that’s well below average in transition.

selection.label: Dylan Harper Over 15.5 Total Points + Assists -111

node.writeup: We aren't expecting the Spurs to show any less mercy tonight vs. a depleted Warriors side than they did last night vs. the shorthanded Lakers, who were crushed 136-108 at Crypto.com.  At the moment this isn't the Golden State we have come to know, either, with Jimmy Butler and Steph Curry both out, Buddy Hield and Jonathan Kuminga just traded, and Kristaps Porzingis not expected to make his Warriors debut until after the All-Star break.  Might Steve Kerr have to suit up for the Dubs? Wemby in particular seems to be ramping up his game, scoring 40 in just 26 minutes last night in LA and letting Mitch Johnson know he would have liked to stay on the floor longer!  Play Spurs

FanDuel. Coming off a 6-assist effort on 13 potentials, I love Nique Clifford on this plus-odds line. With the Kings completely depleted, Clifford’s minutes floor is sky high (37 last game). And he’s facing a Jazz team that allows the most assists per game. In an on-ball role, look for Clifford to continue to drive and kick in an effort to find his teammates tonight.

node.writeup: Brice Sensabaugh is averaging 17.9 points over his last 13 games. That’s despite only averaging a modest 26 minutes a night. Keyonte George (ankle) won’t play Wednesday and I’d be surprised if we see Lauri Markkanen or Jaren Jackson Jr. take the floor in the fourth quarter. Sensabaugh should at least approach 25 minutes against a Kings team that has the third-worst defensive rating in the league and is missing several starters. Combine that with this game being played in Utah and Sensabaugh could stay hot in the scoring department.

expertStreaks.label: 39-17 in Last 56 NBA Player Props Picks

node.writeup: The Heat are going to be very shorthanded Wednesday. Tyler Herro (ribs), Norman Powell (back) and Pelle Larsson (elbow) have all been ruled out. Andrew Wiggins (toe) is also listed as questionable. Jaime Jaquez Jr. is already averaging 5.4 rebounds and 4.7 assists in 29 minutes per game this season. He has combined for at least 10 rebounds and assists in 10 of his last 14 games. The Pelicans have allowed the sixth-most rebounds per game and the fourth-most assists per game in the league, so with Jaquez likely in line to log at least 30 minutes, this over is appealing.

node.writeup: Trey Murphy III has a great matchup against the Heat, who play at the fastest pace in the league. He comes into this contest having averaged 29.5 points and 5.5 assists over his last four games. The Pelicans sent Jose Alvarado to the Knicks at the trade deadline, which has left Murphy with the ball in his hands even more. The last time Murphy faced the Heat, he produced 27 points and five assists in Miami. This rematch will be played in New Orleans, where Murphy shoots 49.3% from the field. I like his chances of hitting this over.

node.writeup: Interpreting how to play this zig-zag is open to interpretation, as the Clippers lost last night...but managed a spread cover in a 102-95 setback.  In the bigger picture, the Rockets have become increasingly unreliable, covering just 5 of their last 21 games, that big hammer we saw early in the season having disappeared.  It seems a case of rinse-and-repeat lately for the Rockets, as whenever Kevin Durant gets double-teamed or denied in the 4th Q, Houston's offense seems to struggle.  The Clippers still await James Harden successor Darius Garland in the backcourt, but ex-Pacer Bennedict Mathurin made his LA debut last night, and as long as Kawhi Leonard is on the floor (he scored 24 points last night), the Clips merit consideration.  Play Clippers

The New York Knicks are coming off possibly their worst loss of the season in last night’s defeat to the Pacers. It will be a tough spot tonight to face Philadelphia, but this is also a 76ers team returning from a five game West coast road trip. They showed poor defense in the final game giving up 135 points to Portland. Look for Bridges to also be motivated as his scoring has dipped each game this year against Philadelphia, including a season worst 19 percent shooting in the last matchup.

node.writeup: The 76ers are back home, where Tyrese Maxey averages 7.2 assists and 3.9 rebounds. New York is playing the second night of a back-to-back, following an OT game no less, and won't have OG Anunoby again. (Though Mitchell Robinson will return). Look for Maxey to clear this prop total for the third time in four meetings with the Knicks this season.

node.writeup: The Pistons are the better team easily, but super-thin at center with Jalen Duren (17.7 PPG, 10.4 RPG) and Isaiah Stewart (10.0 PPG, 5.1 RPG) out suspended. The Raptors, meanwhile, have been a vastly better team with No. 1 center Jakob Poeltl in the lineup. He hadn't played since Dec. 21 but will tonight. Certainly his minutes will be somewhat limited, but he'll also then get the next week off so maybe not crazy low. Poeltl's return likely hurts at least some Sandro Mamukelashvili Over props tonight.
